title: OXO SteeL Muddler with Non-Scratch Nylon Head and Soft Non-Slip Grip, Silver, 9-Inch
description:
About this item
Perfect for muddling fresh herbs, citrus fruits and more
Generous nylon head won’t damage glass
Sturdy, stainless steel body
Soft, comfortable non-slip grip
reviews_5s:
Prepare to be empowered! This is much more than your average bludgeoning hand extension. This is the perfect marriage of form and function, quantity and quality, not to mention the bang you get for your buck. A balanced design, ergonomically shaped handle that is the perfect size whether you are muddling in your mixer or directly in your drinking cup. Buy it!Mixology is my husbands newest hobby so we try to get nice quality tools for him to try out all of the fun cocktail recipes we find! This is a nice sturdy muddler that is easy to clean and looks nice with our bar tools.Does the job even in a glass! Good value!I bought this muddler so that I could make caipirinha, the national drink of Brazil. I am not Brazilian, and barely know anyone who has ever been to Brazil. But we tried caipirinha at a restaurant and were hooked, and wanted to make them at home. I tried crushing the lime pieces with a spoon, and the drink just didn't have the same zingy taste as in the restaurant. A friend advised me that a muddler was the key to success. I went with OXO because I have had good experiences with many of their other products: well designed, durable, comfortable grips.
All of those characteristics apply here. The OXO Steel Muddler is comfortable to hold, you can really grab it hard and mash down on the lime in the glass. The bottom is coated so you don't break anything while you are mashing, and the bottom also has teeth-like indentations cut into it so you get a good smashing while allowing liquid to squeeze out. Cleaning it is very easy, I do not use the dishwasher because it is so simple to wash.
Our caipirinhas taste great. Thank you OXO and Amazon!This thing is sturdy and feels like it'll last forever. I don't muddle a whole lot but when I am this muddler feels great.Very nice quality muddler.I like having a stash of frozen concentrated OJ around the house so I can make a quick pitcher whenever the mood strikes. This muddler is perfect for mashing and mixing that big frozen lump of concentrate into perfect, instant OJ.TerrificI love mojitos and my partner makes a fine one using this efficient and easy to clean muddler. Love that it’s easy to clean and easy on the glassware.Made a gin drink w muddled cherries! Worked great and easy to clean. Has teeth to macerate the cherries perfectly. 🍒
reviews_1s:
I'm a cocktail fan and Mojito is one of my favorite and have been making it for the past couple of years using wooden muddlers. I decided to buy OXO Steel Muddler, since wood muddler's don't last long enough and after a while lacquer wears off to add another ingredient to a Mojito.

Initially I thought of buying Swissmar muddler but positive reviews in Amazon for the OXO muddler and having decent experiences with other OXO products made me to opt for this OXO muddler but I think I made a wrong choice, this muddler broke into two on the first day of use. Unlike most other steel muddlers, which have all steel single handle, this OXO muddler handle is comprised of two parts - upper half that has the head with steel and the lower half with plastic and i don't think the join between them is sturdy because handle broke exactly on the join.

In my opinion and experience, this OXO muddler is poorly constructed and is not strong enough to even muddle mint. I'm returning this to amazon and plan to buy a steel muddler with all steel handle.How do you mess up a muddler's design? It's just a blunt stick. You apply pressure to mash things that you put between it and a glass.

This muddler only mashed things successfully for me a handful of times before snapping in half. Seems to be a faulty joint between the metal and plastic halves... the little screw doesn't go very deep. It's happened to others here in the reviews as well. (I wasn't really putting much lateral pressure on the thing, or abusing it in any way.) Usually Oxo makes pretty good products, but not this time.

I'm going to get a single-material muddler next time I venture out and buy one. In the meantime, my pestle (from my mortar+pestle) works pretty well as a substitute muddler.The metal cap on one end fell off almost immediately. Tried contacting customer support for a replacement and it's impossible to get a hold of anyone. I used to love OXO products, but they just lost a customer.Very disappointed in this product. I was making mojitos and didn't seem the muddler was great. I have all of oxo products in my kitchen so I was surprised, love them all!I should have read the other reviews here. Today I dropped it and the metal cap broke off. Too bad because it's the perfect shape and worked well otherwise.After about a year of use the metal cap fell off the top of the muddler leaving a rough surface. Tried to glue it back on. Glue won't hold it in place. We are ordering a new muddler that does not have a separate piece to fall off.Sadly this muddler fell apart after a couple years' light use (see pictures).

I own a *lot* of products from Oxo and most of them are great, but can't recommend this one. I think it just has too many pieces attached together in too many places (I saw some other pictures of muddlers which broke in a different place).Great at first, then it broke in two after a few weeks. The top part is plastic, it's not stainless all the way through. Oxo is usually good, but this one is a dud.I WAS VERY EXCITED TO PURCHASE THE OXO MUDDLER AND START USING IT AS SOON AS POSSIBLE. I EVEN ELECTED THE OVERNIGHT DELIVERY OPTION SO THAT I COULD USE IT RIGHT AWAY!! I HAVE USED IT 3 TIMES AND THE PLASTIC TIP STRIPPED OUT FROM THE SCREW THAT HOLDS IT IN PLACE. IT IS NOT MUCH FUN TO TRY AND FISH THE TIP OUT OF A GLASS FULL OF MUDDLED MINT AND LIME!! I AM NOW GOING TO LOOK FOR A MUDDLER THAT IS ALL ONE PIECE AND WILL NEVER BUY THIS PRODUCT AGAINNOT RECOMMENDED - Mine broke after just a few uses. Usually I like the OXO brand products, but this one doesn't measue up.
